instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 3 Maths.
output:
# Grade 3 Mathematics (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Numbers to 500, place value
- Week 2: Addition and subtraction within 500
- Week 3: Multiplication (2, 3, 4, 5, 10 tables)
- Week 4: Division (grouping and sharing)
- Week 5: Money (shopping problems)
- Week 6: Geometric patterns
- Week 7: 2D shapes (properties)
- Week 8: Measurement (length)
- Week 9: Data handling (tally charts)
- Week 10: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Numbers to 750, ordering
- Week 2: Addition and subtraction with carrying
- Week 3: Multiplication (repeated addition)
- Week 4: Division (repeated subtraction)
- Week 5: Fractions (halves, quarters, eighths)
- Week 6: Time (analogue and digital)
- Week 7: 3D objects (naming and sorting)
- Week 8: Measurement (mass)
- Week 9: Data handling (bar graphs)
- Week 10: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Numbers to 1000, place value
- Week 2: Mental mathematics (doubling and halving)
- Week 3: Multiplication (multi-step problems)
- Week 4: Division (long division intro)
- Week 5: Fractions (sharing equally)
- Week 6: Time (calendars)
- Week 7: Symmetry (line of symmetry)
- Week 8: Measurement (capacity)
- Week 9: Data handling (interpreting data)
- Week 10: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Numbers to 1000, counting patterns
- Week 2: Addition and subtraction (large numbers)
- Week 3: Multiplication and division (inverse operations)
- Week 4: Fractions (problem solving)
- Week 5: Money (profit and loss)
- Week 6: Geometry (position and movement)
- Week 7: Measurement (temperature)
- Week 8: Data handling (project work)
- Week 9: Final Revision
- Week 10: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-setswana.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/mathematics-setswana.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-xitsonga.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/mathematics-xitsonga.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-isizulu.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/mathematics-isizulu.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-english.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/mathematics-english.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-sesotho.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/mathematics-sesotho.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 3 English.
output:
# Grade 3 English Home Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Phonics (vowel digraphs), Listening & Speaking (news)
- Week 2: Reading (fiction), Writing (personal recount)
- Week 3: Phonics (consonant blends), Handwriting
- Week 4: Language Structures (nouns & pronouns)
- Week 5: Listening & Speaking (giving instructions)
- Week 6: Reading (poetry), Writing (simple poem)
- Week 7: Phonics (silent 'e'), Vocabulary building
- Week 8: Language Structures (verbs: present tense)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Phonics (long vowel sounds), Reading (non-fiction)
- Week 2: Writing (informational paragraph)
- Week 3: Language Structures (adjectives)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (storytelling)
- Week 5: Reading (fables), Writing (dialogue)
- Week 6: Phonics (suffixes -ed, -ing)
- Week 7: Language Structures (conjunctions)
- Week 8: Vocabulary (synonyms and antonyms)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Phonics (prefixes), Reading (drama/plays)
- Week 2: Writing (short script)
- Week 3: Language Structures (past tense)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (reporting)
- Week 5: Reading (informational texts), Writing (lists)
- Week 6: Phonics (complex sounds), Spelling
- Week 7: Language Structures (prepositions)
- Week 8: Vocabulary (dictionary skills)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Phonics (revision), Reading (longer stories)
- Week 2: Writing (narrative essay)
- Week 3: Language Structures (punctuation)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (presentation)
- Week 5: Reading (comics), Writing (captions)
- Week 6: Vocabulary (idiomatic expressions)
- Week 7: Language Structures (future tense)
- Week 8: Revision
- Week 9: Final Revision
- Week 10: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-afrikaans.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/home-languages-afrikaans.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-isixhosa.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/home-languages-isixhosa.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-draft-siswati.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/second-additional-language-draft-siswati.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-english.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/mathematics-english.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-draft-xitsonga.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade3/second-additional-language-draft-xitsonga.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 3 Natural sciences.
output:
# Grade 3 Natural Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Living things (Characteristics)
- Week 2: Animals (Classification)
- Week 3: Plants (Parts and functions)
- Week 4: Habitats and ecosystems
- Week 5: Life cycles of animals
- Week 6: Food chains
- Week 7: Biodiversity
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Matter and materials (Solids)
- Week 2: Matter and materials (Liquids)
- Week 3: Matter and materials (Gases)
- Week 4: Changes of state (Melting/Freezing)
- Week 5: Properties of materials
- Week 6: Natural vs processed materials
- Week 7: Recycling materials
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Energy (Sources of energy)
- Week 2: Electricity (Basic circuits)
- Week 3: Magnetism
- Week 4: Light and shadows
- Week 5: Sound and vibration
- Week 6: Forces (Push and pull)
- Week 7: Simple machines
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Earth and beyond (The Sun)
- Week 2: The Moon and its phases
- Week 3: The Stars and constellations
- Week 4: Planet Earth (Structure)
- Week 5: Soil and rocks
- Week 6: Water cycle
- Week 7: Conservation
- Week 8: Final Assessment

# Graad 6 Afrikaans Eerste Addisionele Taal (KABV)

## Kwartaal 1
- Week 1: Luister en praat (Groete en nuus)
- Week 2: Lees en kyk (Kort stories)
- Week 3: Skryf en ontwerp (Basiese sinne)
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Selfstandige naamwoorde)
- Week 5: My familie en vriende
- Week 6: Diere en hul maniere
- Week 7: Klanke en spelling
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 2
- Week 1: My skool en klaskamer
- Week 2: Lees van feitelike tekste
- Week 3: Skryf van aanwysings
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Werkwoorde)
- Week 5: Kos en lekkergoed
- Week 6: Seisoene en aktiwiteite
- Week 7: Mondelinge voordrag
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 3
- Week 1: Gesondheid en veiligheid
- Week 2: Lees van gedigte
- Week 3: Skryf van kort briewe
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Byvoeglike naamwoorde)
- Week 5: Vervoer en reis
- Week 6: Plekke in die stad
- Week 7: Luister na dwalings (Tales)
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 4
- Week 1: Kultuur en tradisies
- Week 2: Lees van advertensies
- Week 3: Skryf van uitnodigings
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Voorsetsels)
- Week 5: Sport en speletjies
- Week 6: Mondelinge storievertelling
- Week 7: Hersiening
- Week 8: Finale Assessering

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 Sepedi.
output:
# Grade 6 Sepedi First Additional Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Madume le boitsebišo (Advanced greetings and social language)
- Week 2: Go bala dikanegelo tše telejana (Longer stories)
- Week 3: Go ngwala ditemana tša go hlalosa (Descriptive paragraphs)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Maina, Madiri le Mašala)
- Week 5: Polelo ya mmele le go phela gabotse (Body and health)
- Week 6: Meloko le ditswala (Family and community relations)
- Week 7: Go theeletša direto le dikoša (Praises and songs)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 2
- Week 1: Tikologo le tlhago (Environment and nature)
- Week 2: Go bala dikanegelo tša go ruta (Fables and morals)
- Week 3: Go ngwala ditaelo tše hlakaneletšego (Complex instructions)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Mašito le Maatla)
- Week 5: Dipalangwa le maeto (Transport and travel)
- Week 6: Boemo bja boso le bophelo (Weather and life)
- Week 7: Go bolela (Debates and discussions)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 3
- Week 1: Dijo le go kwišiša (Food and comprehension)
- Week 2: Go bala ditaba tša sekolong (School news)
- Week 3: Go ngwala mangwalo a semmušo (Formal letters)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Mahlatse le Makgao)
- Week 5: Dipapadi le boitšhidullo (Sports and exercise)
- Week 6: Dišupatsela le diporo (Directions and maps)
- Week 7: Go theeletša dikanegelo tša setšo (Traditional stories)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 4
- Week 1: Setšo le bophelo bja batho (Culture and human life)
- Week 2: Go bala dipoelo tša tsebo (Informational texts)
- Week 3: Go ngwala direto (Poetry writing)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Revision of grammar)
- Week 5: Boitshwaro le melao (Ethics and laws)
- Week 6: Go bolela ka molomo (Final oral presentations)
- Week 7: Boitokišetšo bja ditlhahlobo (Exam prep)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko ya mafelelo (Final assessment)

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/home-languages-sepedi.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/second-additional-language-sepedi.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: first-additional-language-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/first-additional-language-sepedi.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 Arts culture.
output:
# Grade 6 Arts & Culture (CAPS)

## Term 1: Visual Arts & Music
- Week 1: Visual Arts (Perspective and depth)
- Week 2: Visual Arts (Colour and emotion)
- Week 3: Visual Arts (Drawing: Architectural forms)
- Week 4: Music (Rhythm: Time signatures 2/4, 3/4, 4/4)
- Week 5: Music (Melody: Scales and intervals)
- Week 6: Music (Musical forms: Binary and Ternary)
- Week 7: Integrating Art and Music
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2: Drama & Dance
- Week 1: Drama (Vocal modulation: Pitch and pace)
- Week 2: Drama (Physical expression: Still images and tableau)
- Week 3: Drama (Role play: Historical events)
- Week 4: Dance (Warm-up and strength exercises)
- Week 5: Dance (Rhythmic movement: Polyrhythms)
- Week 6: Dance (Modern South African dance styles)
- Week 7: Choreographing a themed dance
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3: Visual Arts & Drama
- Week 1: Visual Arts (3D Construction: Environmental art)
- Week 2: Visual Arts (Printmaking techniques)
- Week 3: Visual Arts (Painting: Abstract forms)
- Week 4: Drama (Improvisation: Responding to stimuli)
- Week 5: Drama (Script writing: Developing a plot)
- Week 6: Drama (Performance: One-act play)
- Week 7: Integrating Art and Drama
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Music & Dance
- Week 1: Music (Musical instruments: Global context)
- Week 2: Music (Listening to orchestral music)
- Week 3: Music (Final collaborative project)
- Week 4: Dance (Ballet and Ballroom basics)
- Week 5: Dance (Using dynamics and expression)
- Week 6: Dance (Final performance rehearsals)
- Week 7: Final Arts Showcase
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 Life skills.
output:
# Grade 6 Life Skills (CAPS)

## Term 1: Personal and Social Well-being (PSW)
- Week 1: Positive self-esteem (Influences)
- Week 2: Abilities and interests
- Week 3: Peer pressure and social groups
- Week 4: Child abuse (Safety and protection)
- Week 5: Physical Education (Basic fitness/Athletics)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Drama: Mime and movement)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Drawing/Painting)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: Health and Environment
- Week 1: Nutrition (Food groups and balanced meals)
- Week 2: Environmental health (Safe water/Waste management)
- Week 3: Communicable diseases (HIV/AIDS and TB)
- Week 4: Safety in and around water
- Week 5: Physical Education (Dance/Rhythmic movement)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Music: Instrumental/Voice)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Sculpture/3D Art)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Rights and Responsibilities
- Week 1: National symbols and diversity
- Week 2: Religious festivals and beliefs
- Week 3: Gender equality and inclusivity
- Week 4: Children's rights and responsibilities
- Week 5: Physical Education (Invasion games/Teamwork)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Drama: Role play/Puppetry)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Collage/Mosaic)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: Careers and Safety
- Week 1: World of work (Careers and subjects)
- Week 2: Safety in the environment (Traffic/Disasters)
- Week 3: Substance abuse (Causes and effects)
- Week 4: Physical Education (Swimming/Summer games)
- Week 5: Creative Arts (Music: Traditional/Modern)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Design/Media)
- Week 7: Final Revision
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: life-skills.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/life-skills.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 Social sciences.
output:
# Grade 6 Social Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1: Geography - Map skills
- Week 1: Latitude and longitude (Degrees/Minutes)
- Week 2: Scale and distance on maps
- Week 3: Compass directions and bearings
- Week 4: Contours and height
- Week 5: Relief maps of Africa
- Week 6: Maps of the World (Continents/Oceans)
- Week 7: Reading and interpreting various maps
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: History - An African kingdom: Great Zimbabwe
- Week 1: Great Zimbabwe (Background and context)
- Week 2: Social organization and leadership
- Week 3: Trade and external contacts
- Week 4: The decline of Great Zimbabwe
- Week 5: European exploration of Africa (Reasons)
- Week 6: The Portuguese and the Spice Trade
- Week 7: Impact of early exploration
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Geography - Trade and Climate
- Week 1: Why people trade
- Week 2: Imports and exports in South Africa
- Week 3: Trade and transport (Routes)
- Week 4: Fair Trade principles
- Week 5: Climate around the world (Major zones)
- Week 6: Vegetation in different climate zones
- Week 7: Climate change and its impact
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: History - Democracy and Citizenship in South Africa
- Week 1: The road to democracy (Apartheid revision)
- Week 2: The 1994 elections
- Week 3: The South African Constitution
- Week 4: The Bill of Rights
- Week 5: Role of citizens in a democracy
- Week 6: National symbols and unity
- Week 7: Final Revision
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: social-sciences.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/social-sciences.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 Maths.
output:
# Grade 6 Mathematics (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Ordering and comparing up to 9 digits)
- Week 2: Whole numbers (Addition and subtraction)
- Week 3: Common fractions (Addition and subtraction)
- Week 4: Numeric patterns (Flow diagrams)
- Week 5: Whole numbers (Multiplication by 3-digit numbers)
- Week 6: Whole numbers (Division by 2-digit numbers)
- Week 7: Time (Calculations and time zones)
- Week 8: 2D shapes (Properties and naming)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Mental math and estimation)
- Week 2: Addition and subtraction (9-digit numbers)
- Week 3: Decimal fractions (Introduction and notation)
- Week 4: Length (Measurement and calculations)
- Week 5: Mass (Measurement and calculations)
- Week 6: Properties of 2D shapes (Transformations)
- Week 7: Numeric patterns (Input/Output)
- Week 8: Whole numbers (Multiplication)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Whole numbers (9-digit multiplication)
- Week 2: Whole numbers (9-digit division)
- Week 3: Common fractions (Adding and subtracting fractions)
- Week 4: Mass and Capacity (Revision)
- Week 5: Viewing objects (3D perspectives)
- Week 6: Properties of 3D objects
- Week 7: Data handling (Pictographs and Pie charts)
- Week 8: Geometric patterns
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Mental math and estimation)
- Week 2: Number sentences (Inverse operations)
- Week 3: Common fractions (Problem solving)
- Week 4: Perimeter, Area, and Volume
- Week 5: Position and movement (Grid maps)
- Week 6: Probability (Scale of probability)
- Week 7: Data handling (Summarizing data)
- Week 8: Final Revision
- Week 9: Final Assessment
- Week 10: Final Feedback

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: mathematics.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/mathematics.pdf)

- Week 3: Common fractions (Addition and subtraction)
- Week 4: Numeric patterns (Flow diagrams)
- Week 5: Whole numbers (Multiplication by 3-digit numbers)
- Week 6: Whole numbers (Division by 2-digit numbers)
- Week 7: Time (Calculations and time zones)
- Week 8: 2D shapes (Properties and naming)
- Week 9: Revision

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 English.
output:
# Grade 6 English Home Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Group discussion: Social issues)
- Week 2: Reading (Short story), Writing (Character study)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Tenses: Perfect tense)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Persuasive speech)
- Week 5: Reading (Poetry), Writing (Free verse poem)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Adjectives and Adverbs: Degrees of comparison)
- Week 7: Reading (Mythology/Legends), Writing (Original legend)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Punctuation: Semi-colons and Brackets)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Interviewing skills)
- Week 2: Reading (Newspaper article), Writing (Feature article)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Phrases and Clauses)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Debate: Formal rules)
- Week 5: Reading (Drama), Writing (Play scene)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Modal verbs)
- Week 7: Reading (Informational text), Writing (Summary/Precis)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Conjunctions: Complex sentences)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Storytelling with dialogue)
- Week 2: Reading (Historical novel), Writing (Diary of a character)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Active and Passive voice)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Panel discussion)
- Week 5: Reading (Technical instructions), Writing (User manual)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Direct and Indirect speech - revision)
- Week 7: Reading (Comics/Graphic novels), Writing (Storyboarding)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Punctuation: Elision and Apostrophe)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Keynote presentation)
- Week 2: Reading (Autobiographies), Writing (Personal narrative)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Revision of all grammar)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Role play: Mediation)
- Week 5: Reading (Advertising), Writing (Marketing plan/Ad)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Figurative language)
- Week 7: Final Revision (Exam focus)
- Week 8: Final Examination
- Week 9: Feedback and Moderation
- Week 10: End of year celebration

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-afrikaans.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/home-languages-afrikaans.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-isixhosa.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/home-languages-isixhosa.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-isizulu.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/second-additional-language-isizulu.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: caps-errata-african-languages.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/caps-errata-african-languages.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: first-additional-language-isindebele.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/first-additional-language-isindebele.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 6 Natural sciences.
output:
# Grade 6 Natural Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1: Life and Living
- Week 1: Photosynthesis (Plants and food)
- Week 2: Nutrients in food (The five food groups)
- Week 3: Nutrition and a balanced diet
- Week 4: Ecosystems and food webs
- Week 5: Interdependence in ecosystems
- Week 6: Animal and plant adaptations
- Week 7: Biodiversity in our local area
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: Matter and Materials
- Week 1: Solids, liquids, and gases (Revision)
- Week 2: Mixtures (Dissolving and filtering)
- Week 3: Soluble and insoluble substances
- Week 4: Separating mixtures (Technology link)
- Week 5: Processing materials (Raw to processed)
- Week 6: Properties of materials (Metals vs Non-metals)
- Week 7: Strengthening structures (Bridges)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Energy and Change
- Week 1: Electric circuits (Components)
- Week 2: Conductors and insulators
- Week 3: Systems and control (Electrical)
- Week 4: Safety with electricity
- Week 5: Mains electricity in our homes
- Week 6: Renewable and non-renewable energy
- Week 7: Conserving energy
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: Earth and Beyond
- Week 1: The Solar System (Planets and Sun)
- Week 2: Movements of the Earth and Moon
- Week 3: Telescopes and space observation
- Week 4: Systems for looking into space
- Week 5: Space exploration history
- Week 6: Soil and rocks (Sedimentary)
- Week 7: Fossils and the history of life on Earth
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: natural-sciences-and-technology.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade6/natural-sciences-and-technology.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Afrikaans.
output:
# Graad 4 Afrikaans Eerste Addisionele Taal (KABV)

## Kwartaal 1
- Week 1: Luister en praat (Groete en nuus)
- Week 2: Lees en kyk (Kort stories)
- Week 3: Skryf en ontwerp (Basiese sinne)
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Selfstandige naamwoorde)
- Week 5: My familie en vriende
- Week 6: Diere en hul maniere
- Week 7: Klanke en spelling
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 2
- Week 1: My skool en klaskamer
- Week 2: Lees van feitelike tekste
- Week 3: Skryf van aanwysings
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Werkwoorde)
- Week 5: Kos en lekkergoed
- Week 6: Seisoene en aktiwiteite
- Week 7: Mondelinge voordrag
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 3
- Week 1: Gesondheid en veiligheid
- Week 2: Lees van gedigte
- Week 3: Skryf van kort briewe
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Byvoeglike naamwoorde)
- Week 5: Vervoer en reis
- Week 6: Plekke in die stad
- Week 7: Luister na dwalings (Tales)
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 4
- Week 1: Kultuur en tradisies
- Week 2: Lees van advertensies
- Week 3: Skryf van uitnodigings
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Voorsetsels)
- Week 5: Sport en speletjies
- Week 6: Mondelinge storievertelling
- Week 7: Hersiening
- Week 8: Finale Assessering

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Sepedi.
output:
# Grade 4 Sepedi First Additional Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Go theeletša le go bolela (Greetings and social language)
- Week 2: Go bala le go kwišiša (Short stories)
- Week 3: Go ngwala le go hlama (Simple paragraphs)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Nouns)
- Week 5: Mareo a sekolo (School terminology)
- Week 6: Diphoofolo (Animals and their sounds)
- Week 7: Go theeletša (Songs and rhymes)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 2
- Week 1: Polelo ya gae (Home environment)
- Week 2: Go bala (Non-fiction texts)
- Week 3: Go ngwala (Instructions)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Verbs)
- Week 5: Mehlare le dijalo (Trees and plants)
- Week 6: Boemo bja leratadima (Weather)
- Week 7: Go bolela (Role play)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 3
- Week 1: Dijo le go phela gabotse (Food and health)
- Week 2: Go bala (Folk tales)
- Week 3: Go ngwala (Creative stories)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Adjectives)
- Week 5: Dipalangwa (Means of transport)
- Week 6: Dipapadi (Sports and games)
- Week 7: Go theeletša (Poetry)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 4
- Week 1: Setšo sa rena (Our culture)
- Week 2: Go bala (News articles)
- Week 3: Go ngwala (Letters)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Revision)
- Week 5: Boitshwaro (Behaviour and ethics)
- Week 6: Dithoko (Traditional praises)
- Week 7: Boitokišetšo bja ditlhahlobo (Exam prep)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko ya mafelelo (Final assessment)

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/home-languages-sepedi.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/second-additional-language-sepedi.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: first-additional-language-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/first-additional-language-sepedi.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Arts culture.
output:
# Grade 4 Arts & Culture (CAPS)

## Term 1: Visual Arts & Drama
- Week 1: Visual Arts (Line and shape)
- Week 2: Visual Arts (Drawing our environment)
- Week 3: Visual Arts (Colour theory: Primary & Secondary)
- Week 4: Drama (Mime and gesture)
- Week 5: Drama (Vocal exercises and projection)
- Week 6: Drama (Storytelling through role-play)
- Week 7: Integrating Art and Drama
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2: Music & Dance
- Week 1: Music (Rhythm and tempo)
- Week 2: Music (Musical instruments: Woodwind & Brass)
- Week 3: Music (Singing in harmony)
- Week 4: Dance (Basic locomotor movements)
- Week 5: Dance (Coordination and balance)
- Week 6: Dance (Traditional South African dances)
- Week 7: Choreographing a short sequence
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3: Visual Arts & Music
- Week 1: Visual Arts (Texture and pattern)
- Week 2: Visual Arts (Collage and 3D construction)
- Week 3: Visual Arts (Painting techniques)
- Week 4: Music (Dynamics: Loud and Soft)
- Week 5: Music (Graphic notation)
- Week 6: Music (Composing simple melodies)
- Week 7: Integrating Art and Music
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Drama & Dance
- Week 1: Drama (Improvisation)
- Week 2: Drama (Creating characters)
- Week 3: Drama (Short play production)
- Week 4: Dance (Modern and contemporary steps)
- Week 5: Dance (Using space and levels)
- Week 6: Dance (Final performance rehearsals)
- Week 7: Final Showcase
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Life skills.
output:
# Grade 4 Life Skills (CAPS)

## Term 1: Personal and Social Well-being (PSW)
- Week 1: Development of the self (Self-esteem)
- Week 2: Emotions and feelings
- Week 3: Peer pressure
- Week 4: Bullying (Identification and prevention)
- Week 5: Physical Education (Locomotor movements)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Drama: Mime)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Drawing)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: Health and Environment
- Week 1: Nutrition (The five food groups)
- Week 2: Personal and environmental hygiene
- Week 3: HIV and AIDS education
- Week 4: Safety in and around water
- Week 5: Physical Education (Rhythm and dance)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Music: Percussion)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Painting)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Rights and Responsibilities
- Week 1: Children's rights and responsibilities
- Week 2: Cultural diversity in South Africa
- Week 3: Respecting the body (Privacy)
- Week 4: Dealing with conflict
- Week 5: Physical Education (Games and activities)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Drama: Role play)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Sculpture)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: Careers and Safety
- Week 1: Knowledge of the world of work
- Week 2: Traffic safety
- Week 3: Substance abuse education
- Week 4: Physical Education (Athletics/Team sports)
- Week 5: Creative Arts (Music: Traditional songs)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Design)
- Week 7: Final Revision
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: life-skills.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/life-skills.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Social sciences.
output:
# Grade 4 Social Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1: Geography - Places where people live
- Week 1: People and places
- Week 2: Landmarks and directions
- Week 3: Maps and plan views
- Week 4: Grid references
- Week 5: Why people live where they do
- Week 6: Rural and urban areas
- Week 7: Jobs people do in different places
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: History - Finding out about the past
- Week 1: Introduction to history
- Week 2: Local history (Our school and area)
- Week 3: Learning from stories (Oral history)
- Week 4: Learning from objects and buildings
- Week 5: Changes over time in our community
- Week 6: Family history and traditions
- Week 7: Historical figures in our area
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Geography - Map skills and Food
- Week 1: Maps and symbols
- Week 2: The map of South Africa (Provinces)
- Week 3: Where our food comes from
- Week 4: Farming in South Africa
- Week 5: Food processing (Farm to factory)
- Week 6: Healthy eating (Link to Life Skills)
- Week 7: Sustainable farming
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: History - Learning from Leaders
- Week 1: What makes a good leader?
- Week 2: Nelson Mandela: His early life
- Week 3: Nelson Mandela: His leadership
- Week 4: Mahatma Gandhi: His life and message
- Week 5: Other South African leaders
- Week 6: Leadership in our school/community
- Week 7: Final Revision
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: social-sciences.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/social-sciences.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Maths.
output:
# Grade 4 Mathematics (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Ordering and comparing)
- Week 2: Number sentences (Addition and subtraction)
- Week 3: Whole numbers (Addition and subtraction)
- Week 4: Common fractions (Diagrams and comparison)
- Week 5: Length (Measurement and conversion)
- Week 6: 2D shapes (Naming and properties)
- Week 7: Data handling (Collect and organize)
- Week 8: Numeric patterns (Input/Output)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Multiplication basics)
- Week 2: Whole numbers (Division basics)
- Week 3: Number sentences (Multiplication and division)
- Week 4: Time (Analogue and digital)
- Week 5: Properties of 2D shapes
- Week 6: Data handling (Bar graphs)
- Week 7: Numeric patterns (Sequences)
- Week 8: Capacity and Volume
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Whole numbers (4-digit addition/subtraction)
- Week 2: Whole numbers (2-digit multiplication)
- Week 3: Whole numbers (2-digit division)
- Week 4: Common fractions (Adding fractions)
- Week 5: Mass (Measurement)
- Week 6: Viewing objects (Top/Side/Front)
- Week 7: Geometric patterns
- Week 8: Properties of 3D objects
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Mental math/Rounding)
- Week 2: Addition and subtraction (Large numbers)
- Week 3: Multiplication and division (Revision)
- Week 4: Common fractions (Problem solving)
- Week 5: Perimeter and Area (Intro)
- Week 6: Data handling (Interpreting graphs)
- Week 7: Probability (Intro)
- Week 8: Final Revision
- Week 9: Final Assessment
- Week 10: Final Feedback

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: mathematics.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/mathematics.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 English.
output:
# Grade 4 English Home Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Group discussion)
- Week 2: Reading (Short story), Writing (Personal letter)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Sentence structure)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Oral presentation)
- Week 5: Reading (Poetry), Writing (Poem)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Verbs: Present tense)
- Week 7: Reading (Folklore), Writing (Story rewrite)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Pronouns)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Interview skills)
- Week 2: Reading (News article), Writing (Simple report)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Adjectives and Adverbs)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Debating)
- Week 5: Reading (Play script), Writing (Dialogue)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Past tense)
- Week 7: Reading (Informational text), Writing (List/Summary)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Conjunctions)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Telling a joke/story)
- Week 2: Reading (Novel excerpt), Writing (Narrative essay)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Prepositions)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Book review)
- Week 5: Reading (Instructional text), Writing (Recipe/Guide)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Future tense)
- Week 7: Reading (Comic strips), Writing (Speech bubbles)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Punctuation: Quotation marks)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Formal speech)
- Week 2: Reading (Biography), Writing (Self-profile)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Subject-Verb agreement)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Radio advertisement)
- Week 5: Reading (Advertisements), Writing (Poster)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Direct and Indirect speech)
- Week 7: Final Revision (All grammar)
- Week 8: Final Examination
- Week 9: Feedback and Moderation
- Week 10: End of Term activities

## Curriculum Resources

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 4 Natural sciences.
output:
# Grade 4 Natural Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1: Life and Living
- Week 1: Living and non-living things
- Week 2: Structure of plants
- Week 3: What plants need to grow
- Week 4: Animals and their skeletons
- Week 5: Animal habitats
- Week 6: Food chains
- Week 7: Biodiversity in South Africa
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: Matter and Materials
- Week 1: Materials around us
- Week 2: Solids, liquids, and gases
- Week 3: Changes of state (Water)
- Week 4: The water cycle
- Week 5: Raw and processed materials
- Week 6: Properties of materials
- Week 7: Strengthening materials (Technology link)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Energy and Change
- Week 1: Energy for life (Food)
- Week 2: Energy transfer (Circuits)
- Week 3: Energy from the Sun
- Week 4: Input and Output energy
- Week 5: Movement and energy (Forces)
- Week 6: Heat and light energy
- Week 7: Sound energy
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: Earth and Beyond
- Week 1: Planet Earth (Our home)
- Week 2: The Sun and its importance
- Week 3: The Moon and its movement
- Week 4: Planets in our solar system
- Week 5: Telescopes and space exploration
- Week 6: Soil and rocks
- Week 7: Conservation of Earth's resources
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: natural-sciences-and-technology.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade4/natural-sciences-and-technology.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Afrikaans.
output:
# Graad 7 Afrikaans Eerste Addisionele Taal (KABV)

## Kwartaal 1
- Week 1: Luister en praat (Groete en nuus)
- Week 2: Lees en kyk (Kort stories)
- Week 3: Skryf en ontwerp (Basiese sinne)
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Selfstandige naamwoorde)
- Week 5: My familie en vriende
- Week 6: Diere en hul maniere
- Week 7: Klanke en spelling
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 2
- Week 1: My skool en klaskamer
- Week 2: Lees van feitelike tekste
- Week 3: Skryf van aanwysings
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Werkwoorde)
- Week 5: Kos en lekkergoed
- Week 6: Seisoene en aktiwiteite
- Week 7: Mondelinge voordrag
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 3
- Week 1: Gesondheid en veiligheid
- Week 2: Lees van gedigte
- Week 3: Skryf van kort briewe
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Byvoeglike naamwoorde)
- Week 5: Vervoer en reis
- Week 6: Plekke in die stad
- Week 7: Luister na dwalings (Tales)
- Week 8: Assessering

## Kwartaal 4
- Week 1: Kultuur en tradisies
- Week 2: Lees van advertensies
- Week 3: Skryf van uitnodigings
- Week 4: Taalstrukture (Voorsetsels)
- Week 5: Sport en speletjies
- Week 6: Mondelinge storievertelling
- Week 7: Hersiening
- Week 8: Finale Assessering

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Sepedi.
output:
# Grade 7 Sepedi First Additional Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Madume le polelo ya leago (Social language and formal greetings)
- Week 2: Go bala dikanegelo (Reading short stories)
- Week 3: Go ngwala ditemana tša go hlalosa (Descriptive writing)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Maina le Maatla)
- Week 5: Bophelo le maphelo (Health and wellness)
- Week 6: Polelo ya baswa (Youth language and culture)
- Week 7: Go theeletša direto (Traditional praises)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 2
- Week 1: Tikologo le tšhomišo ya matlotlo (Environment and resources)
- Week 2: Go bala dikanegelo tša go ruta (Fables and lessons)
- Week 3: Go ngwala mangwalo a semmušo (Formal letters)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Mašito le Mašala)
- Week 5: Dipalangwa le kgokagano (Transport and communication)
- Week 6: Boemo bja boso (Weather and climate change)
- Week 7: Go bolela (Debating current issues)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 3
- Week 1: Dijo le meetlo (Food and traditions)
- Week 2: Go bala ditaba tša naga (National news)
- Week 3: Go ngwala direto (Writing poetry)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Grammar: Mahlatse le Makgao)
- Week 5: Dipapadi le boitšhidullo (Sports and exercise)
- Week 6: Melao le ditshwanelo (Laws and rights)
- Week 7: Go theeletša dikanegelo tša setšo (Traditional stories)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko (Assessment)

## Term 4
- Week 1: Setšo le boitšhupo (Culture and identity)
- Week 2: Go bala dipoelo tša tsebo (Informational texts)
- Week 3: Go ngwala (Creative writing/Essays)
- Week 4: Sebopego sa polelo (Revision of all grammar)
- Week 5: Bokamoso le ditiro (Future and careers)
- Week 6: Go bolela ka molomo (Final presentations)
- Week 7: Boitokišetšo bja ditlhahlobo (Exam prep)
- Week 8: Tshekatsheko ya mafelelo (Final assessment)

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/home-languages-sepedi.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/second-additional-language-sepedi.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: first-additional-language-sepedi.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/first-additional-language-sepedi.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Arts culture.
output:
# Grade 7 Creative Arts (CAPS)

## Term 1: Visual Arts & Drama
- Week 1: Visual Arts (Observational drawing: Still life)
- Week 2: Visual Arts (Composition and layout)
- Week 3: Visual Arts (Colour theory: Tertiary colours)
- Week 4: Drama (Physical expression: Mime and gesture)
- Week 5: Drama (Voice: Modulation and projection)
- Week 6: Drama (Characterization and improvisation)
- Week 7: Integrating Art and Drama
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2: Music & Dance
- Week 1: Music (Musical notation: Stave and clefs)
- Week 2: Music (Rhythm: Complex time signatures)
- Week 3: Music (Listening to diverse South African genres)
- Week 4: Dance (Warm-up and flexibility training)
- Week 5: Dance (Rhythmic patterns and coordination)
- Week 6: Dance (Traditional South African dance styles)
- Week 7: Choreographing a group sequence
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3: Visual Arts & Music
- Week 1: Visual Arts (3D Construction: Found objects)
- Week 2: Visual Arts (Printmaking: Lino/Stenciling)
- Week 3: Visual Arts (Painting: Contemporary styles)
- Week 4: Music (Composition: Simple melodies)
- Week 5: Music (Musical instruments: Global context)
- Week 6: Music (Collaborative music making)
- Week 7: Integrating Art and Music
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Drama & Dance
- Week 1: Drama (Script writing: Conflict and climax)
- Week 2: Drama (Directing and staging basics)
- Week 3: Drama (Short play performance)
- Week 4: Dance (Contemporary and modern techniques)
- Week 5: Dance (Expression and performance skills)
- Week 6: Dance (Final production rehearsals)
- Week 7: Final Creative Arts Showcase
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Life skills.

# Grade 7 Life Orientation (CAPS)

## Term 1: Development of the self in society
- Week 1: Self-image and self-motivation
- Week 2: Interests, abilities, and potential
- Week 3: Peer pressure and group work
- Week 4: Bullying: Prevention and response
- Week 5: Physical Education (Basic fitness/Games)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Drama: Mime and storytelling)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Painting)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: Health, Social and Environmental Responsibility
- Week 1: Nutrition and a balanced diet
- Week 2: Communicable diseases (HIV/AIDS)
- Week 3: Environmental health (Cleanliness and waste)
- Week 4: Substance abuse education
- Week 5: Physical Education (Rhythmic movement)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Music: African musical instruments)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Drawing)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Constitutional Rights and Responsibilities
- Week 1: Human rights and the Bill of Rights
- Week 2: Cultural diversity and religious festivals
- Week 3: National symbols and ceremonies
- Week 4: Democratic participation
- Week 5: Physical Education (Athletics/Summer sports)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Drama: Role play)
- Week 7: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Sculpture)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: World of Work and Safety
- Week 1: Careers and subject choices
- Week 2: Time management and study skills
- Week 3: Traffic safety and passenger behavior
- Week 4: Physical Education (Swimming/Games)
- Week 5: Creative Arts (Music: Folk and modern songs)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Visual Arts: Design/Media)
- Week 7: Final Revision
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Social sciences.
output:
# Grade 7 Social Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1: Geography - Map skills
- Week 1: Local maps and street maps
- Week 2: Sketch maps and landmarks
- Week 3: Latitude and longitude (Degrees and minutes)
- Week 4: Map scales and distance calculations
- Week 5: Relief and contours
- Week 6: Orthophoto maps
- Week 7: Interpreting diverse map types
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: History - The Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade
- Week 1: Introduction to slavery
- Week 2: West Africa before the slave trade
- Week 3: The nature of the slave trade (Middle Passage)
- Week 4: Impact of slavery on West Africa
- Week 5: Impact of slavery on the Americas
- Week 6: Resistance to slavery
- Week 7: Abolition of the slave trade
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Geography - Earthquakes and Volcanoes
- Week 1: Structure of the Earth
- Week 2: Plate tectonics
- Week 3: Causes and effects of earthquakes
- Week 4: Measuring earthquakes (Richter scale)
- Week 5: Causes and types of volcanoes
- Week 6: Living with volcanoes and earthquakes
- Week 7: Case studies of natural disasters
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: History - Colonization of the Cape
- Week 1: Indigenous people of the Cape (Khoisan)
- Week 2: The Dutch East India Company (VOC) at the Cape
- Week 3: Impact of colonization on indigenous people
- Week 4: Slave labor at the Cape
- Week 5: Frontiers and conflict in the Cape Colony
- Week 6: Significance of Cape history today
- Week 7: Final Revision
- Week 8: Final Assessment

- [CAPS Document: social-science.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/social-science.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Maths.
output:
# Grade 7 Mathematics (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Revision and properties)
- Week 2: Exponents (Squares, cubes, roots)
- Week 3: Geometry of 2D shapes (Naming and classifying)
- Week 4: Geometry of 2D shapes (Congruency and similarity)
- Week 5: Common fractions (Calculations and problem solving)
- Week 6: Decimal fractions (Calculations and problem solving)
- Week 7: Integers (Counting, ordering and comparing)
- Week 8: Integers (Addition and subtraction)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Numeric and Geometric patterns
- Week 2: Functions and relationships (Input/Output)
- Week 3: Algebraic expressions (Introduction and notation)
- Week 4: Algebraic equations (Solving simple equations)
- Week 5: Area and Perimeter of 2D shapes
- Week 6: Surface area and Volume of 3D objects
- Week 7: Numeric patterns (revision)
- Week 8: Graphs (Interpreting and drawing)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Multiplication and division revision)
- Week 2: Numeric and Geometric patterns (Advanced)
- Week 3: Functions and relationships (Algebraic)
- Week 4: Algebraic expressions (Adding and subtracting)
- Week 5: Algebraic equations (Problem solving)
- Week 6: Geometry of straight lines
- Week 7: Transformation geometry
- Week 8: Data handling (Collect, organize and summarize)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Whole numbers (Revision of all properties)
- Week 2: Integers (Multiplication and division basics)
- Week 3: Common and Decimal fractions (Revision)
- Week 4: Area, Perimeter, Surface Area and Volume (Revision)
- Week 5: Functions and relationships (Revision)
- Week 6: Probability (Introduction to theory)
- Week 7: Data handling (Analysis and reporting)
- Week 8: Final Revision
- Week 9: Final Examination
- Week 10: Feedback and Results

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: wiskunde-mathematics.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/wiskunde-mathematics.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/mathematics.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 English.
output:
# Grade 7 English Home Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Group discussion: Literary themes)
- Week 2: Reading (Novel), Writing (Literary essay intro)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Verbs: Transitive and Intransitive)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Prepared speech: Social issues)
- Week 5: Reading (Poetry: Figurative language), Writing (Sonnet/Lyric poem)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Phrases and Clauses: Relative clauses)
- Week 7: Reading (Folklore/Myths), Writing (Modern adaptation of a myth)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Punctuation: Colons and Semicolons)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Interviewing and reporting)
- Week 2: Reading (Newspaper/Magazine), Writing (Investigative report)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Modality: Modal verbs)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Debate: Formal structure)
- Week 5: Reading (Drama: Stage directions/Dialogue), Writing (One-act play scene)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Tenses: Revision of all perfect tenses)
- Week 7: Reading (Informational text), Writing (Summary/Precis)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Active and Passive voice)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Oral presentation with visual aids)
- Week 2: Reading (Short stories), Writing (Narrative essay)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Direct and Indirect speech)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Book review/Panel discussion)
- Week 5: Reading (Visual literacy: Cartoons/Ads), Writing (Ad campaign)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Conjunctions: Compound and Complex sentences)
- Week 7: Reading (Non-fiction: Biographies), Writing (Autobiographical sketch)
- Week 8: Language Structures (Vocabulary: Etymology/Prefixes/Suffixes)
- Week 9: Revision
- Week 10: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Listening & Speaking (Persuasive oral)
- Week 2: Reading (Literature revision: Novel and Drama)
- Week 3: Language Structures (Revision of all grammar and conventions)
- Week 4: Listening & Speaking (Role play: Conflict resolution/Mediation)
- Week 5: Reading (Public documents), Writing (Formal letter/Application)
- Week 6: Language Structures (Figurative language and Rhetoric)
- Week 7: Final Revision (Exam preparation)
- Week 8: Final Examination
- Week 9: Feedback and Moderation
- Week 10: Year-end activities

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-afrikaans.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/home-languages-afrikaans.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: home-languages-isixhosa.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/home-languages-isixhosa.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: second-additional-language-isizulu.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/second-additional-language-isizulu.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: caps-errata-african-languages.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/caps-errata-african-languages.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: first-additional-language-isindebele.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/first-additional-language-isindebele.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 7 Natural sciences.
output:
# Grade 7 Natural Sciences (CAPS)

## Term 1: Life and Living
- Week 1: The Biosphere (Concept and structure)
- Week 2: Biodiversity (Classification of living things)
- Week 3: Sexual reproduction in plants
- Week 4: Human reproduction (Overview)
- Week 5: Variation and adaptation
- Week 6: Human systems (Digestive/Respiratory)
- Week 7: Microorganisms (Health impact)
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 2: Matter and Materials
- Week 1: Properties of materials (Solids, liquids, gases)
- Week 2: Separation of mixtures (Physical methods)
- Week 3: Acids, bases, and neutrals
- Week 4: Introduction to the Periodic Table
- Week 5: Metals and non-metals
- Week 6: Processing of materials
- Week 7: Sustainability in material use
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 3: Energy and Change
- Week 1: Sources of energy (Renewable/Non-renewable)
- Week 2: Potential and Kinetic energy
- Week 3: Heat transfer (Conduction, Convection, Radiation)
- Week 4: Insulation and energy efficiency
- Week 5: Energy and the national grid
- Week 6: Electricity (Circuits and safety)
- Week 7: Visible light and color
- Week 8: Revision & Assessment

## Term 4: Earth and Beyond
- Week 1: Relationship of the Sun to Earth
- Week 2: Relationship of the Moon to Earth
- Week 3: Tides and their causes
- Week 4: Historical development of astronomy
- Week 5: Modern space exploration
- Week 6: Earth's atmosphere
- Week 7: Global warming and climate change
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: natural-science.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/natural-science.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: natuurwetenskappe-natural-science.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/natuurwetenskappe-natural-science.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: technology.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/technology.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: tegnologie-technology.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade7/tegnologie-technology.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade R Sepedi.
output:
# Grade R Sepedi First Additional Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Madume le boitsebišo (Greetings)
- Week 2: Ke nna mang? (Who am I?)
- Week 3: Go theeletša dikanegelo (Listening to stories)
- Week 4: Dikoša tša bana (Children's songs)
- Week 5: Mmele wa ka (My body)
- Week 6: Mebala (Colors)
- Week 7: Go hlaola dilo (Sorting objects)
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Lapa la ka (My family)
- Week 2: Dijo (Food)
- Week 3: Go bala dikanegelo ka seswantšho (Picture stories)
- Week 4: Diphoofolo tša gae (Domestic animals)
- Week 5: Diaparo (Clothes)
- Week 6: Go ngwala (Emergent writing)
- Week 7: Metshameko (Games)
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Sekolo sa ka (My school)
- Week 2: Dinomoro (Numbers 1-5)
- Week 3: Diphoofolo tša naga (Wild animals)
- Week 4: Boemo bja boso (Weather)
- Week 5: Dipalangwa (Transport)
- Week 6: Maphelo (Health)
- Week 7: Direto (Poetry)
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Go ithuta (Learning)
- Week 2: Mekete (Celebrations)
- Week 3: Ditlhaba (Nature)
- Week 4: Poledišano (Conversation)
- Week 5: Go itokišetša Grade 1 (Transition)
- Week 6: Bokamoso (Future)
- Week 7: Boitokišetšo bja mafelelo
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade R Arts culture.
output:
# Grade R Arts & Culture (Integrated)

## Term 1: Visual Arts & Music
- Week 1: Drawing with crayons and pencils
- Week 2: Painting with fingers and brushes
- Week 3: Exploring colors (Primary colors)
- Week 4: Listening to different sounds
- Week 5: Clapping rhythms and beats
- Week 6: Singing nursery rhymes
- Week 7: Making shakers and drums
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2: Drama & Dance
- Week 1: Moving like animals (Dance)
- Week 2: Role play: Dressing up
- Week 3: Using puppets to tell stories
- Week 4: Action songs and movement
- Week 5: Exploring space (High and Low)
- Week 6: Creative storytelling
- Week 7: Group dance and coordination
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3: Visual Arts & Drama
- Week 1: Clay and playdough modeling
- Week 2: Cutting and pasting (Collage)
- Week 3: Making masks
- Week 4: Drama: Expressing feelings
- Week 5: Still images and tableau
- Week 6: Creating an art gallery
- Week 7: Dramatic play in the play area
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Music & Dance
- Week 1: Traditional songs and dances
- Week 2: Musical games (Musical chairs)
- Week 3: Final class performance
- Week 4: Free creative expression
- Week 5: Art from recycled materials
- Week 6: Music and relaxation
- Week 7: Arts showcase
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade R Life skills.
output:
# Grade R Life Skills (CAPS)

## Term 1: Personal and Social Well-being
- Week 1: Me and my classroom
- Week 2: Personal hygiene (Washing hands)
- Week 3: Emotions (Happy, Sad, Angry)
- Week 4: Manners and sharing
- Week 5: Physical Education (Big muscle play)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Painting and messy play)
- Week 7: Safety in the classroom
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2: Health and Environment
- Week 1: Healthy eating (Fruit and veg)
- Week 2: My senses (Taste, Smell, Touch)
- Week 3: Safety at home
- Week 4: Water play and conservation
- Week 5: Physical Education (Balance and coordination)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Playdough and construction)
- Week 7: The environment (Plants and trees)
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3: Community and Rights
- Week 1: People who help us (Doctors, Police)
- Week 2: Religious and cultural festivals
- Week 3: National symbols (The flag)
- Week 4: My rights and responsibilities
- Week 5: Physical Education (Ball skills)
- Week 6: Creative Arts (Singing and dancing)
- Week 7: Animals and their babies
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Careers and Safety
- Week 1: Jobs people do
- Week 2: Road safety (Traffic lights)
- Week 3: Danger and fire safety
- Week 4: Physical Education (Outdoor games)
- Week 5: Creative Arts (Puppetry and drama)
- Week 6: Celebration and transition to Grade 1
- Week 7: Year-end fun
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade R Maths.
output:
# Grade R Mathematics (CAPS)

## Term 1: Numbers and Counting
- Week 1: Introduction to numbers 1-5 (Counting objects)
- Week 2: Recognising number symbols 1-3
- Week 3: Comparing collections (More/Less)
- Week 4: Sorting by color and size
- Week 5: Basic shapes (Circles and Squares)
- Week 6: Position (On top of, under)
- Week 7: Patterns (AB patterns with colors)
- Week 8: Revision and play-based assessment

## Term 2: Operations and Patterns
- Week 1: Numbers 1-10 (Counting forwards)
- Week 2: Number symbols 4-6
- Week 3: Simple addition (Adding one more)
- Week 4: Shapes (Triangles and Rectangles)
- Week 5: Measurement (Long and Short)
- Week 6: Time (Morning, Afternoon, Night)
- Week 7: Complex patterns (AABB)
- Week 8: Revision and play-based assessment

## Term 3: Space and Shape
- Week 1: Number symbols 7-10
- Week 2: Counting backwards from 5
- Week 3: Sharing (Equal groups)
- Week 4: 3D objects (Balls and Boxes)
- Week 5: Measurement (Heavy and Light)
- Week 6: Data handling (Sorting by kind)
- Week 7: Directions (Left and Right)
- Week 8: Revision and play-based assessment

## Term 4: Consolidation
- Week 1: Numbers 1-10 (Fluency)
- Week 2: Recognising symbols in the environment
- Week 3: Problem solving (Simple story sums)
- Week 4: Shapes and Patterns (Revision)
- Week 5: Capacity (Full and Empty)
- Week 6: Money (Recognising coins)
- Week 7: Year-end mathematics fun
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-grade-r-setswana.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grader/mathematics-grade-r-setswana.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-grade-r-english.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grader/mathematics-grade-r-english.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-grade-r-sesotho.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grader/mathematics-grade-r-sesotho.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-grade-r-xitsonga.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grader/mathematics-grade-r-xitsonga.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-grade-r-afrikaans.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grader/mathematics-grade-r-afrikaans.pdf)

: Numbers and Counting

- Week 2: Recognising number symbols 1-3
- Week 3: Comparing collections (More/Less)
- Week 4: Sorting by color and size

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade R English.
output:
# Grade R English Home Language (CAPS)

## Term 1: Listening and Speaking
- Week 1: Classroom routines and greetings
- Week 2: Listening to short stories
- Week 3: Following simple instructions
- Week 4: Emergent Reading (Handling a book)
- Week 5: Phonological Awareness (Rhyming words)
- Week 6: Visual Literacy (Looking at pictures)
- Week 7: Vocabulary building (My Body)
- Week 8: Assessment (Oral)

## Term 2: Emergent Literacy
- Week 1: Identifying initial sounds
- Week 2: Storytelling (Retelling a story)
- Week 3: Emergent Writing (Scribbling and drawing)
- Week 4: Letter-sound recognition (a, s, t)
- Week 5: Fine motor skills (Tracing)
- Week 6: Vocabulary (My Home)
- Week 7: Group discussion (My Family)
- Week 8: Assessment (Oral and fine motor)

## Term 3: Reading and Writing
- Week 1: Recognising own name
- Week 2: Matching letters and sounds
- Week 3: Reading environmental print (Signs)
- Week 4: Creative writing (Drawing a story)
- Week 5: Vocabulary (Animals)
- Week 6: Segmenting words into syllables
- Week 7: Poetry and Action songs
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Consolidation
- Week 1: Advanced phonological awareness
- Week 2: "Reading" familiar books
- Week 3: Writing simple letters and patterns
- Week 4: Role play and drama
- Week 5: Vocabulary (Transport)
- Week 6: Preparing for Grade 1 (Transition)
- Week 7: Year-end literacy fun
- Week 8: Final Assessment

## Curriculum Resources

# Grade R Natural Sciences (Integrated)

## Term 1: Exploration
- Week 1: Exploring with our senses
- Week 2: Living vs Non-living things
- Week 3: Parts of a plant
- Week 4: Water in our world
- Week 5: Weather observation
- Week 6: Exploring materials (Hard/Soft)
- Week 7: Sounds around us
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 2: Discovery
- Week 1: Growing plants (Seeds)
- Week 2: Animals and their homes
- Week 3: Day and Night
- Week 4: The Sun and Heat
- Week 5: Sinking and Floating
- Week 6: Exploring magnets
- Week 7: Healthy environment
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 3: Investigation
- Week 1: Insects and small crawly things
- Week 2: Different types of soil
- Week 3: Changing materials (Cooking/Melting)
- Week 4: Light and Shadows
- Week 5: Air and Wind
- Week 6: Conservation (Recycling)
- Week 7: Body systems (Heartbeat/Breathing)
- Week 8: Assessment

## Term 4: Consolidation
- Week 1: Seasons review
- Week 2: Animal life cycles (Butterflies/Frogs)
- Week 3: Space (Stars and Moon)
- Week 4: Technology in our homes
- Week 5: Electricity safety
- Week 6: Scientific fun experiments
- Week 7: Year-end review
- Week 8: Final Assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 1 Life skills.
output:
# Grade 1 Life Skills (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Myself & my family
- Week 2: Safety at home
- Week 3: Healthy eating
- Week 4: Physical education (basic movement)
- Week 5: Creative arts (drawing)
- Week 6: Environment awareness
- Week 7: Music & rhythm
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: My community
- Week 2: Safety at school
- Week 3: Hygiene practices
- Week 4: Physical education (games)
- Week 5: Creative arts (painting)
- Week 6: Recycling
- Week 7: Music & singing
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: My country
- Week 2: Road safety
- Week 3: Healthy lifestyle
- Week 4: Physical education (teamwork)
- Week 5: Creative arts (crafts)
- Week 6: Environmental care
- Week 7: Music & instruments
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: World cultures
- Week 2: Safety outdoors
- Week 3: Emotional well-being
- Week 4: Physical education (sports)
- Week 5: Creative arts (performance)
- Week 6: Environmental projects
- Week 7: Music & dance
- Week 8: Revision & final assessment

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 1 Maths.
output:
# Grade 1 Mathematics (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Counting 1–10, number recognition
- Week 2: Addition within 10
- Week 3: Subtraction within 10
- Week 4: Shapes and patterns
- Week 5: Measurement (length, mass)
- Week 6: Time (days of week)
- Week 7: Money (coins)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Place value (tens and ones)
- Week 2: Addition within 20
- Week 3: Subtraction within 20
- Week 4: Fractions (halves, quarters)
- Week 5: Geometry (2D shapes)
- Week 6: Measurement (capacity)
- Week 7: Data handling (pictographs)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Counting to 50
- Week 2: Multiplication (groups of 2, 5)
- Week 3: Division (sharing equally)
- Week 4: Fractions (thirds)
- Week 5: Geometry (3D shapes)
- Week 6: Measurement (time: hours)
- Week 7: Data handling (bar graphs)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Counting to 100
- Week 2: Addition/subtraction with regrouping
- Week 3: Multiplication (groups of 10)
- Week 4: Division (remainders)
- Week 5: Geometry (symmetry)
- Week 6: Measurement (temperature)
- Week 7: Probability (everyday events)
- Week 8: Revision & final assessment

## Curriculum Resources
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-setswana.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade1/mathematics-setswana.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-xitsonga.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade1/mathematics-xitsonga.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-isizulu.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade1/mathematics-isizulu.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-english.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade1/mathematics-english.pdf)
- [CAPS Document: mathematics-sesotho.pdf](../../../caps/pdf/grade1/mathematics-sesotho.pdf)

instruction: Generate a CAPS-aligned teaching plan overview for Grade 1 English.
output:
# Grade 1 English Home Language (CAPS)

## Term 1
- Week 1: Phonics (a, e, i, o, u)
- Week 2: Reading short words
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (story retelling)
- Week 4: Writing simple sentences
- Week 5: Vocabulary building
- Week 6: Grammar (nouns)
- Week 7: Creative writing (short story)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 2
- Week 1: Phonics (consonant blends)
- Week 2: Reading comprehension (short passages)
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (dialogues)
- Week 4: Writing (paragraphs)
- Week 5: Grammar (verbs)
- Week 6: Vocabulary expansion
- Week 7: Creative writing (poems)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 3
- Week 1: Phonics (long vowels)
- Week 2: Reading comprehension (stories)
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (debates)
- Week 4: Writing (letters)
- Week 5: Grammar (adjectives)
- Week 6: Vocabulary (synonyms/antonyms)
- Week 7: Creative writing (descriptive)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

## Term 4
- Week 1: Phonics (complex sounds)
- Week 2: Reading comprehension (non-fiction)
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (presentations)
- Week 4: Writing (reports)
- Week 5: Grammar (punctuation)
- Week 6: Vocabulary (idioms)
- Week 7: Creative writing (narrative)
- Week 8: Revision & final assessment

instruction: What is covered in Term 1 for Grade 1 English according to the teaching plan?
output:
In Term 1 for Grade 1 English, the following topics are covered:
- Week 1: Phonics (a, e, i, o, u)
- Week 2: Reading short words
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (story retelling)
- Week 4: Writing simple sentences
- Week 5: Vocabulary building
- Week 6: Grammar (nouns)
- Week 7: Creative writing (short story)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

instruction: What is covered in Term 2 for Grade 1 English according to the teaching plan?

- Week 7: Creative writing (poems)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

instruction: What is covered in Term 3 for Grade 1 English according to the teaching plan?
output:
In Term 3 for Grade 1 English, the following topics are covered:
- Week 1: Phonics (long vowels)
- Week 2: Reading comprehension (stories)
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (debates)
- Week 4: Writing (letters)
- Week 5: Grammar (adjectives)
- Week 6: Vocabulary (synonyms/antonyms)
- Week 7: Creative writing (descriptive)
- Week 8: Revision & assessment

instruction: What is covered in Term 4 for Grade 1 English according to the teaching plan?
output:
In Term 4 for Grade 1 English, the following topics are covered:
- Week 1: Phonics (complex sounds)
- Week 2: Reading comprehension (non-fiction)
- Week 3: Listening & speaking (presentations)
- Week 4: Writing (reports)
- Week 5: Grammar (punctuation)
- Week 6: Vocabulary (idioms)
- Week 7: Creative writing (narrative)
- Week 8: Revision & final assessment
